creator: mfechner@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to johannes.kunde from comment #4)
sry I cannot reproduce this.
I did a fresh install using this CBSD script in a new jail (branch local):
https://gitlab.fechner.net/mfechner/gitlab-cbsd/-/tree/local?ref_type=heads

on a FreeBSD 13.2-RELEASE-p10 host.

Waited till the installation is finished
(make sure you jail has internet access, this is required e.g. to finish the installation as e.g. yarn is downloading packages).

Set the jail to autostart and rebooted the host system.

The jails was starting fine as well as gitlab:
cbsd jlogin gitlab
gitlab:/usr/local/www/gitlab-ce/log@[15:15] # service gitlab status
The GitLab web server with pid 4383 is running.
The GitLab Sidekiq job dispatcher with pid 4393 is running.
The GitLab Workhorse with pid 4399 is running.
Gitaly with pid 4398 is running.
GitLab and all its components are up and running.

Make sure you installation is ok, you do not have any packages locked or outdated and you executed `pkg autoremove` like it is described in my manual:
https://gitlab.fechner.net/mfechner/Gitlab-docu/-/blob/master/update/16.9-16.10-freebsd.md?ref_type=heads

Maybe you want to go again over all the steps in the upgrade manual.

If you find a way to reproduce this with a fresh jail, please let me know the steps, but as it works for me it sound like you have locked and outdated packages, obsolete packages installed or iocage has some strange problems or something in your configuration is wrong.
